_about: OpenClaw-style provider + model priority chain. Copy to llm_providers.json (gitignored) to use it; get_llm() then tries providers top-to-bottom, and within each provider its models in order, falling through on ANY error (across providers). A paid model is used ONLY if you list AND enable it. Delete the file to fall back to env (OPENROUTER_MODEL).

_fields.name: free label for logs
_fields.type: openrouter | ollama | xai | openai | generic (decides the litellm prefix + default base_url)
_fields.api_key_env: name of the .env var holding the key (omit for keyless providers like local ollama); a provider whose key is missing is skipped, not fatal
_fields.context: optional provider-level default context-window (tokens) for its string models
_fields.models: models IN PRIORITY ORDER. Each is either an id string, or {id, context}. id has NO litellm prefix (e.g. 'openai/gpt-oss-120b:free' for openrouter, 'qwen2.5:7b' for ollama). context = that model's MAX context window in tokens — set it so CrewAI never over-fills a small model (check the model's OpenRouter page; local Ollama is whatever you set in the Ollama app, ~32k). The chain uses the SMALLEST window across all endpoints to size prompts safely.
_fields.enabled: optional false to keep a provider in the file but skip it
